title: Travel agent + Amadeus MCP keychain fix — bare references, OpenAI-only baseline (AMBER round 2 → GREEN)
description: Round 2 of AMBER → GREEN. The SQL fix shipped clean (log_classification works), but the Amadeus HTTP branches in both the travel runtime and the Amadeus MCP server failed with `Illegal header value b'Bearer '` — keychain.amadeus_token.access_token rendered empty. Comparing against the proven-working `repos/e2e/.../amadeus_ai_api.yaml` keychain showed three structural problems: (1) keychain Jinja templates referenced `workload.X` instead of bare `X`, which silently bound to empty strings; (2) Jinja conditionals in the OAuth2 endpoint URL don't render in keychain context; (3) `when:` predicates on per-provider keychain entries appear unsupported, breaking the openai/anthropic/vertex provider switch. Claude has shipped the fixes in working tree, NOT committed: keychain blocks now use bare references, OAuth2 endpoint hardcodes the test URL, anthropic/vertex/ollama keychain entries are removed (multi-provider abstraction returns as a follow-up after this OpenAI-only baseline is GREEN), classify_intent step is OpenAI-only, parse_classification drops the multi-provider response unwrap, Amadeus URL Jinja conditionals collapsed to plain strings, MCP search_flights maxFlightOffers hardcoded to int 10. Codex job: validate, ship 1 ops PR, re-register both playbooks, replay all six smoke phases (terminal flights/locations/help, canvas, MCP search, OpenAI provider). Cap: 1 ops PR.

files_claude_wrote.repos/ops/automation/agents/travel/runti: modified — keychain block rewritten to mirror `repos/e2e/.../amadeus_ai_api.yaml`: bare workload references (`{{ gcp_auth }}`, `{{ amadeus_key_path }}`, etc.), hardcoded OAuth2 endpoint, removed anthropic/vertex/ollama keychain entries (multi-provider returns as follow-up). classify_intent step is OpenAI-only — explicit URL/headers/payload, no provider conditionals. parse_classification step's response-shape unwrap drops the vertex/anthropic branches (still parses OpenAI responses correctly). Amadeus search URLs collapsed from Jinja conditional to plain strings (https://test.api.amadeus.com/v2/shopping/flight-offers and same /v1/reference-data/locations). The render steps still expose `provider` for the status pill; that just renders 'openai' until multi-provider returns.
modified — keychain block uses bare workload references and hardcoded OAuth2 endpoint URL (same fix pattern as the travel runtime). All four Amadeus search URLs (flights, locations, hotels by-city, activities) collapsed from Jinja conditional to plain test-API strings. searchCriteria.maxFlightOffers hardcoded to integer `10` (YAML-quoted Jinja `| int` still renders as a string; the proven amadeus_ai_api pattern is to hardcode).

context_from_amber_round_2.what_passed: log_classification SQL fix (proven), travel help → completed in 4.008s, MCP tools/list (5 tools), MCP get_token (token_bound=true)
context_from_amber_round_2.what_failed: travel flights → failed in amadeus_search_flights with `Request error: Illegal header value b'Bearer '`. Same for travel locations. MCP search_flights/search_locations also failed despite get_token reporting bound. Multi-provider classify_intent failed for anthropic/vertex-ai/ollama (different shapes, but same root cause: empty keychain entries).
context_from_amber_round_2.diagnosis: The MCP get_token step uses `keychain.amadeus_token.access_token is defined` — this is true even when the token's data dict is bound but the access_token string inside it is empty. The HTTP step then renders `Bearer ` (empty) in the Authorization header, which urllib3 rejects.
context_from_amber_round_2.root_cause_per_amber_round_2_result: keychain block templates that use `workload.X` resolve to empty strings; the proven pattern in repos/e2e/.../amadeus_ai_api.yaml uses bare references (`gcp_auth`, `amadeus_key_path`). Jinja conditionals in the OAuth2 endpoint URL also don't render correctly in keychain context.

1_pydantic_validate.objective: Confirm both patched playbooks still validate.
spec: `noetl validate repos/ops/automation/agents/travel/runtime.yaml`
`noetl validate repos/ops/automation/agents/mcp/amadeus.yaml`
Both should validate clean — the changes are surgical (keychain refactor + URL collapse + integer cast).

2_open_and_merge_ops_pr.objective: Branch, commit, push, PR, merge the keychain + URL fixes.
spec: `cd repos/ops && git checkout -b kadyapam/travel-keychain-bare-refs-baseline`.
Stage automation/agents/travel/runtime.yaml + automation/agents/mcp/amadeus.yaml.

Commit using `git commit -F -` heredoc. Conventional-commits: `fix(travel-agent): keychain bare references + OpenAI-only baseline (AMBER unblock)`. Body cites AMBER round 2 result file, points at the proven `amadeus_ai_api.yaml` pattern, lists the three structural fixes (bare refs, hardcoded OAuth2 endpoint, OpenAI-only).
PR title: same.
Push, PR, merge with `gh pr merge --squash --delete-branch`.
2_open_and_merge_ops_pr.verify: PR merged. Capture PR URL + merge SHA.

3_re_register_both_playbooks.objective: Re-register both patched playbooks in the kind catalog.
spec: Pull merged ops at HEAD.
`noetl --server http://192.168.1.240:8082 register repos/ops/automation/agents/travel/runtime.yaml` (now version 3 of the travel runtime).
`noetl --server http://192.168.1.240:8082 register repos/ops/automation/agents/mcp/amadeus.yaml` (version 2 of the Amadeus MCP).
Capture new catalog_ids + versions.

4_smoke_terminal_all_intents.objective: Replay phase 7 with all three intents to status=completed.
spec: In the prompt: `travel help` — expected: completed (regression check, was already passing).

`travel flights from SFO to JFK on 2026-07-15 for 2 adults` — expected: log_classification → amadeus_search_flights returns 200 from Amadeus → render_flights → persist_and_callback → completed. Widget: app:column with title, query echo, status pills, app:carousel of flight cards, rerun button.
`travel locations near Boston` — expected: amadeus_search_locations returns 200 → render_locations → persist_and_callback → completed. Widget: app:recordtable with airports/cities.
Click rerun in any widget; confirm a fresh execution materializes inline.

If Amadeus returns 4xx/5xx (sandbox is intermittently flaky), the agent should still terminate cleanly via render_amadeus_failure with a friendly error widget — capture the friendly-error path if it triggers.
Capture screenshots: (i) flight carousel, (ii) location recordtable, (iii) help widget for regression.
4_smoke_terminal_all_intents.verify: All three intents reach status=completed (or status=completed via the friendly-error render_amadeus_failure path on Amadeus sandbox flakes). Widgets render. Button callbacks work.

spec: Navigate to /travel. Type a flight query. Expected: chat bubble with text + widget below.
Click rerun in the widget; confirm onSubmit re-runs the original query.
Capture screenshot.
5_smoke_canvas.verify: Travel canvas renders the same widget as the prompt.

spec: `cd /mcp/amadeus`; `tools` (5 listed; regression check).
`call get_token` — token_bound=true (regression).
`call search_locations keyword=Boston` — expected: success with locations payload.
`call search_flights origin=SFO destination=JFK departureDate=2026-07-15` — expected: success with flight offers.
`call search_hotels cityCode=NYC` — expected: success with hotels payload (or 4xx if Amadeus sandbox doesn't have NYC hotels — capture either way).
`call search_activities latitude=42.3601 longitude=-71.0589` — expected: success with activities payload.
Screenshots.
6_smoke_amadeus_mcp.verify: All 5 MCP tools dispatch correctly. At least search_flights and search_locations return 200 with non-empty data.

spec: `travel --provider openai flights from SFO to JFK on 2026-07-15` — expected: same as default; explicit-flag run validates the parser.
Note in result file: anthropic/vertex-ai/ollama provider parity is deferred to a follow-up round once this OpenAI baseline is GREEN. The keychain refactor in this round removed those entries.
7_smoke_openai_provider_baseline.verify: OpenAI explicit-flag works; failure modes for the deferred providers are captured cleanly (e.g. 'Unknown provider' or empty Bearer).

8_bump_ai_meta_pointer.objective: Bump repos/ops gitlink in ai-meta on top of existing 16 unpushed commits.
spec: `cd /Volumes/X10/projects/noetl/ai-meta && git submodule update --remote repos/ops`.
`git add repos/ops`.
Commit: `chore(sync): bump ops for travel-agent + Amadeus-MCP keychain fix (AMBER → GREEN)`.
Don't push ai-meta — Kadyapam pushes.
8_bump_ai_meta_pointer.verify: ai-meta has a fresh local commit on top of existing unpushed history.

expected_state.description: Both playbooks patched + reregistered. Travel agent flights and locations executions complete with widgets. MCP search_flights and search_locations return non-empty results. Travel canvas mirrors prompt. ai-meta gitlink staged.
expected_state.verify_shell: set -eo pipefail; cd /Volumes/X10/projects/noetl/ai-meta; BAD_REFS=$(grep -c 'workload.gcp_auth' repos/ops/automation/agents/travel/runtime.yaml repos/ops/automation/agents/mcp/amadeus.yaml 2>/dev/null | grep -v ':0' | wc -l); BAD_URLS=$(grep -c 'amadeus_env == .test.' repos/ops/automation/agents/travel/runtime.yaml repos/ops/automation/agents/mcp/amadeus.yaml 2>/dev/null | grep -v ':0' | wc -l); BARE_REFS=$(grep -c 'auth: "{{ gcp_auth }}"' repos/ops/automation/agents/travel/runtime.yaml 2>/dev/null || echo 0); echo "bad_refs:$BAD_REFS bad_urls:$BAD_URLS bare_refs:$BARE_REFS"; if [ "$BAD_REFS" -eq 0 ] && [ "$BAD_URLS" -eq 0 ] && [ "$BARE_REFS" -ge 1 ]; then echo 'GOAL: TRAVEL_KEYCHAIN_BARE_REFS_LANDED'; else echo 'GOAL: NOT_YET'; fi

denied_tools: Modifying repos/noetl, repos/gui, repos/docs, repos/e2e — single ops fix
Tweaking Claude's playbook beyond obvious typos
Re-introducing multi-provider keychain in this round (defer until baseline is GREEN)
preferred_tools: git apply / git commit -F - / git push (one topic branch in repos/ops only)
gh pr create / gh pr merge --squash --delete-branch
result_file_instructions: Write bridge/outbox/20260509-054530-travel-keychain-amber-to-green.result.json. Required fields: {id, from: 'codex', to: 'claude-cowork', completed_at, overall_status, phases: {1_pydantic_validate: {travel_validates, mcp_validates}, 2_open_and_merge_ops_pr: {pr_url, merged_sha}, 3_re_register_both_playbooks: {travel_catalog_id, travel_version, mcp_catalog_id, mcp_version}, 4_smoke_terminal_all_intents: {help_status, flights_execution_id, flights_status, locations_execution_id, locations_status, button_callbacks_work, screenshots: []}, 5_smoke_canvas: {execution_id, widget_visible, button_callback, screenshot}, 6_smoke_amadeus_mcp: {tools_listed: 5, get_token_bound, search_locations_ok, search_flights_ok, search_hotels_ok, search_activities_ok, screenshots: []}, 7_smoke_openai_provider_baseline: {explicit_flag_works}, 8_bump_ai_meta_pointer: {ops_sha, ai_meta_commit_local}}, regressions_found, iterations, verdict, notes, deferred_followups: ['Multi-provider keychain (anthropic/vertex/ollama) re-introduced once baseline is GREEN', 'Phase 2: travel agent uses Amadeus MCP server internally', 'Wire hotels and activities branches in the travel agent (currently fall through to render_help)', 'app:form widget for refining filters before re-running']}. If verdict GREEN, append a paragraph to bridge/outbox/codex-spike-green-validation.md noting the flagship round flipped from AMBER to GREEN.
